name: civil_infrastructure_emergency_exemption_mesa_ind_col
premise: A Civil Infrastructure response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving civil infrastructure emergency exemption mesa ind col. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as individual vs collective under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Elliot Hughes
agents.identity: I am Elliot Hughes, accountable for a defensible call in Civil Infrastructure when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays collective for individual.

agents.name: Nadia Navarro
agents.identity: I am Nadia Navarro, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Civil Infrastructure.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so collective isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of individual.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect collective.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to collective to secure individual under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Elliot Hughes must decide under individual vs collective. What does Elliot Hughes do?
